methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
CX2 Industries is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by Nathan Mintz and Porter Smith that has raised $31M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $31M total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical for Series A to subsequent-stage progression, yielding $62M. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M, producing a median implied valuation of $300M and a range of $150M–$500M.

The two methods produce a wide triangulated range of $53M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $181M. This dispersion reflects material uncertainty around CX2's relative market position and growth profile within the Defense & Security peer set. The gap between stage-progression ($62M) and peer-median ($300M) methods suggests the critical unknowns are CX2's competitive differentiation relative to named peers and the sustainability of recent fundraising momentum. Resolution of product maturity, customer concentration, and defensibility claims would materially narrow the range.
